item.title: Novo Nordisk Oral Semaglutide for Adolescents
item.description:
• Summary: Novo Nordisk is seeking approval for oral semaglutide (the active ingredient in Rybelsus) in adolescents with type 2 diabetes. This move comes amid a competitive landscape for obesity and diabetes pills. Oral semaglutide is currently approved for adults, and an adolescent indication would expand the patient population. The company is also developing higher-dose formulations for obesity. The article emphasizes the potential approval timeline and the ongoing duel with other oral GLP-1 receptor agonists.
  • MoAs: GLP-1 receptor agonist: Activates glucagon-like peptide-1 receptors, stimulating insulin secretion, suppressing glucagon release, slowing gastric emptying, and promoting satiety.
  • Sources: FiercePharma

item.title: Roche Obesity Amylin Drug
item.description:
• Summary: Roche is maintaining confidence in its amylin-based obesity drug, asserting it remains valuable for patients who want to avoid common side effects associated with other obesity treatments. The drug targets the amylin receptor and may offer an alternative for weight management.
  • MoAs: Amylin receptor agonist
  • Sources: BioSpace

item.title: Diabetes Management Next Era
item.description:
• Summary: The article explores the next era of diabetes management, highlighting advances in drug delivery systems, novel glucose-lowering agents such as GLP-1 receptor agonists and SGLT2 inhibitors, and improved continuous glucose monitoring technologies.
  • MoAs: GLP-1 receptor agonists, SGLT2 inhibitors, insulin analogs, and other glucose-lowering therapies.
  • Sources: BioSpace

item.title: Novo Nordisk Oral Semaglutide for Kids
item.description:
• Summary: Novo Nordisk is developing an oral formulation of semaglutide for pediatric use, aiming to treat type 2 diabetes and obesity in children. The article also touches on Altimmune's $225 million offering and a partnership between Merck and Google Cloud.
  • MoAs: GLP-1 receptor agonist - semaglutide.
  • Sources: Endpoints News
